title: Has / Have / Had / Will Have
description: Master the four forms of 'Have' — Has (singular present), Have (plural present), Had (past), Will Have (future). Learn as main verb (possession) and as helping verb (perfect tenses). Essential for everyday English.

subtopics.title: Has — Singular Present
subtopics.description: She has a car. He has two brothers. Third person singular present.

subtopics.title: Have — Plural Present
subtopics.description: I have, You have, We have, They have — possession in present.

subtopics.title: Had — Past Tense
subtopics.description: I had a dog. She had a fever. Past possession and past perfect.

subtopics.title: Will Have — Future
subtopics.description: I will have a new job. She will have finished by then. Future forms.

subtopics.title: Have as Main Verb (Possession)
subtopics.description: I have a pen. She has time. Have meaning 'to possess or experience'.

subtopics.title: Have as Helping Verb (Perfect)
subtopics.description: I have eaten. She has gone. Had finished. Perfect tense formation.

subtopics.title: In Questions
subtopics.description: Do you have? Does she have? Have you eaten? Had they left?

subtopics.title: In Negatives
subtopics.description: I don't have, She doesn't have, I haven't, She hadn't — negatives.

subtopics.title: Have Got / Has Got
subtopics.description: I've got a problem. She's got talent. British English informal form.

subtopics.title: Had in Questions & Negatives
subtopics.description: Had you eaten? I hadn't seen him. Past perfect questions and negatives.

subtopics.title: Will Have in Questions & Negatives
subtopics.description: Will you have finished? I won't have time. Future perfect forms.

subtopics.title: In Daily Life
subtopics.description: 100+ real sentences using has/have/had in daily situations.

subtopics.title: In Professional English
subtopics.description: I have completed the project. She has experience. Office use.

subtopics.title: Common Errors
subtopics.description: He have a car (wrong!), I has finished (wrong!) — fixing mistakes.

learningObjectives: Use has correctly with third person singular (he/she/it)
Use had for past possession and past perfect tense
Use will have for future perfect and future possession
Distinguish have as main verb vs helping verb
Form questions with do/does + have and have/has alone
Form negatives with don't/doesn't have and haven't/hasn't
Use have got and has got in informal contexts
Use had in past perfect questions and negatives
Avoid common subject-verb agreement errors with have/has
